response: ["Take slow, deep breaths using the 4-7-8 technique: inhale for 4 counts, hold for 7, and exhale for 8, which activates your parasympathetic nervous system to counteract the shock response.","Ground yourself immediately by pressing your feet firmly into the floor and naming 5 things you can see, 4 you can touch, 3 you can hear, 2 you can smell, and 1 you can taste to anchor yourself in the present moment.","Step away from the source of distress — turn off the screen, leave the room, or physically remove yourself — giving your nervous system space to begin calming down before processing what you experienced.","Reach out to a trusted friend or family member and talk about what you witnessed, as verbalizing a horrifying experience with a compassionate listener can help your brain begin to process and contextualize the shock.","Engage in gentle physical movement such as a short walk or light stretching to help discharge the adrenaline and stress hormones that have flooded your body, restoring a sense of calm and safety."]
